titleStmt.title: No. 1356 Proposal by the Soviet Delegation This question was raised by Molotov at the Fifth Meeting of the Foreign Ministers, July 22. See ante, p. 227. This subject was referred to the Foreign Ministers at the Sixth Plenary Meeting, July 22. See ante, pp. 246–247.

div.head:
No. 1356
Proposal by the Soviet Delegation This question was raised by Molotov at the Fifth Meeting of the Foreign Ministers, July 22. See ante, p. 227. This subject was referred to the Foreign Ministers at the Sixth Plenary Meeting, July 22. See ante, pp. 246–247.
div.p: Having considered the question of the zone of Tangier we have agreed that this zone which includes the city of Tangier and the area adjacent to it shall, owing to its specific strategic importance, remain international.

Representatives of the states chiefly concerned viz. Great Britain, the United States of America, the U. S. S. R. and France which will be invited to join this declaration will work out an appropriate status of this international zone responding [corresponding?] to the new conditions that resulted from the victory over Hitler Germany.
Spain will be invited to adhere to the new status after the re-establishment of democratic regime in the country.
